headline: Software Engineer | Cloud & DevOps
summary: Software engineer with 8.1 years of experience across web, backend, and cloud systems. Strong fundamentals in software development and system design. I've spent most of my career on web and API development — Python/Django and Node.js mostly. I've been keeping up with AI/ML at a self-learner level — taken some online courses, played with the OpenAI and Anthropic APIs, built a small RAG side project — but I haven't done it in a professional capacity yet. Open to roles where I can either deepen my software engineering work or, if the team is open to it, start contributing to ML-adjacent systems.
skills_text: SQL Airflow MongoDB CSS Six Sigma
career_history_text: Software Engineer Full-stack web application development at a SaaS company. Built React-based admin interfaces and the Node.js REST API backing them. Worked across the stack: frontend components, REST endpoint design, PostgreSQL schema, deployment via Docker/Kubernetes. Comfortable in most parts of a typical web stack though my comfort zone is the backend and database side. Recent learning has been on the testing and CI/CD discipline. DevOps Engineer Cloud infrastructure and DevOps work at an enterprise SaaS company. Owned the AWS account architecture (VPC, IAM, networking), the Terraform modules for our service deployments, and the Kubernetes cluster operations. Designed the CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI + ArgoCD) and the monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki). Strong on the infra and ops side; haven't done much application development. QA Engineer Java backend development at a large enterprise — Spring Boot microservices, Kafka for inter-service messaging, Postgres + Redis for storage. Worked on the customer onboarding flow which involved orchestrating multiple downstream services. Solid on the Spring ecosystem, transaction handling, and the operational side of Java services. Looking to either go deeper on distributed systems or expand into modern application stacks.

Comfortable with the Android framework, Jetpack components, and the typical patterns (MVVM, Hilt, Coroutines). My career has been entirely on mobile so far; interested in expanding into broader backend or platform engineering. Frontend Engineer Cloud infrastructure and DevOps work at an enterprise SaaS company. Owned the AWS account architecture (VPC, IAM, networking), the Terraform modules for our service deployments, and the Kubernetes cluster operations. Designed the CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI + ArgoCD) and the monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki). Strong on the infra and ops side; haven't done much application development.

Headline: Cloud Engineer | Backend systems & APIs
Summary: Software engineer with 2.9 years of experience across web, backend, and cloud systems. Strong fundamentals in software development and system design. My background is full-stack, but my comfort zone is the backend and the database. I've been keeping up with AI/ML at a self-learner level — taken some online courses, played with the OpenAI and Anthropic APIs, built a small RAG side project — but I haven't done it in a professional capacity yet. Open to roles where I can either deepen my software engineering work or, if the team is open to it, start contributing to ML-adjacent systems.
Skills: MongoDB Reinforcement Learning FastAPI Azure PostgreSQL Python Agile Weights & Biases Apache Flink Content Writing OpenSearch Spring Boot
Career History: Cloud Engineer Full-stack web application development at a SaaS company. Built React-based admin interfaces and the Node.js REST API backing them. Worked across the stack: frontend components, REST endpoint design, PostgreSQL schema, deployment via Docker/Kubernetes. Comfortable in most parts of a typical web stack though my comfort zone is the backend and database side. Recent learning has been on the testing and CI/CD discipline.

career_history_text: Cloud Engineer Frontend engineering at a media company. React, TypeScript, and the typical surrounding tooling (Webpack, Jest, Cypress). Built the company's design system from scratch and led the migration from a legacy AngularJS app. Strong on the frontend craft — accessibility, performance, animations — but limited backend exposure. Frontend Engineer Cloud infrastructure and DevOps work at an enterprise SaaS company. Owned the AWS account architecture (VPC, IAM, networking), the Terraform modules for our service deployments, and the Kubernetes cluster operations. Designed the CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI + ArgoCD) and the monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki). Strong on the infra and ops side; haven't done much application development. DevOps Engineer Android mobile development using Java and (more recently) Kotlin at a consumer-app company. Comfortable with the Android framework, Jetpack components, and the typical patterns (MVVM, Hilt, Coroutines). My career has been entirely on mobile so far; interested in expanding into broader backend or platform engineering.

Most of my work is conventional backend engineering — APIs, databases, queues, deployments. I've been keeping up with AI/ML at a self-learner level — taken some online courses, played with the OpenAI and Anthropic APIs, built a small RAG side project — but I haven't done it in a professional capacity yet. Open to roles where I can either deepen my software engineering work or, if the team is open to it, start contributing to ML-adjacent systems.

headline: Software Engineer | Backend systems & APIs
summary: Software engineer with 6.1 years of experience across web, backend, and cloud systems. Strong fundamentals in software development and system design. skills_text: dbt PEFT Snowflake SQL Apache Beam AWS Django Diffusion Models CSS gRPC Image Classification Milvus TypeScript
career_history_text: Software Engineer Full-stack web application development at a SaaS company. Built React-based admin interfaces and the Node.js REST API backing them. Worked across the stack: frontend components, REST endpoint design, PostgreSQL schema, deployment via Docker/Kubernetes. Comfortable in most parts of a typical web stack though my comfort zone is the backend and database side. Recent learning has been on the testing and CI/CD discipline. DevOps Engineer Java backend development at a large enterprise — Spring Boot microservices, Kafka for inter-service messaging, Postgres + Redis for storage. Worked on the customer onboarding flow which involved orchestrating multiple downstream services. Solid on the Spring ecosystem, transaction handling, and the operational side of Java services. Looking to either go deeper on distributed systems or expand into modern application stacks.
